reviews.author: Pavel Verpakhovskiy
reviews.text: recently visited Chùa Hộ Quốc (Thiền Viện Trúc Lâm Hộ Quốc) on Phu Quoc Island, and it was truly remarkable. Perched on a hillside, the temple offers breathtaking sea views and exemplifies traditional Vietnamese architecture with its elegant curved roofs and wooden structures. The three Tam Quan gate arches — symbolising Awakening, Overcoming Dualism, and Liberation — set a contemplative mood right from the entrance. Inside, a profound sense of peace prevails: I spent hours walking around, observing monks, and admiring the majestic jade Buddha statue in the courtyard. Panoramic viewpoints provide stunning vistas of the East Sea — I arrived near sunset, and the golden-pink sky reflected beautifully on the temple. What stood out most was the authentic spiritual atmosphere: this isn’t just a tourist spot, but a living place of worship and meditation. Staff and monks are welcoming, gently reminding visitors about the dress code (covered shoulders and knees). The temple is free to enter and open from 6 am to 6 pm, making it easy to plan a visit at your preferred time — whether for sunrise, daytime contemplation, or sunset views. If you’re on Phu Quoc, Chùa Hộ Quốc is a must‑visit: a place to rest your soul, connect with nature, and experience Vietnam’s spiritual tradition.

reviews.author: Similitude
reviews.text: Absolutely beautiful and peaceful temple! This place is one of the most impressive spots in Phú Quốc. The temple sits on a hill with a stunning sea view — “mountain behind, ocean in front.” The architecture is traditional and elegant, built mostly from wood and stone, giving it a calm, sacred atmosphere. It’s very clean, quiet, and perfect for relaxing or meditation after exploring busy tourist areas. You can take amazing photos from the upper terrace overlooking the ocean. A few tips: wear modest clothes (it’s a temple), bring some water, and avoid going at noon because it can get quite hot. The road up the hill is curvy but well-paved.

reviews.author: Julia Kooper
reviews.text: Ho Quoc Pagoda (Chùa Hộ Quốc – Thiền Viện Trúc Lâm) is one of the most beautiful and popular pagodas on Phú Quốc Island. The temple sits on a hillside overlooking the sea, surrounded by mountains and peaceful nature. Its traditional Vietnamese architecture, dragon stairway, and ocean views make it a great place for photos and quiet reflection. Because it’s easy to reach and included in many tours, the pagoda can get quite busy during the day. It’s still an active monastery, so visitors should dress modestly and stay respectful. The best time to come is early morning for a calm atmosphere and to watch the sunrise over the ocean. Overall, this is a must-see spot combining culture, nature, and spirituality — even if it’s a bit touristic, it remains a breathtaking and meaningful place to visit on Phú Quốc.

reviews.author: Stuart Hammill
reviews.text: Awesome! Arrive early avoiding the coaches. A Grab will happily take you there and wait for a couple of hours for a negotiated return fee. The various structures are fantastic and a def “must do” on the island. There is no entrance fee.

reviews.author: Han Nguyen
reviews.text: Largest temple of Phu Quoc island with a great location, mountain behind and vast ocean in front. The temple was inaugurated in 2012. The landscape on the way to the temple is beautiful and serene.

reviews.author: BOW_KANJANUT
reviews.text: I'm Thai, and I happened to be traveling in Vietnam for my birthday, so I looked for a place to make merit. The temples here are beautiful and very peaceful.

reviews.author: Crafty Fever
reviews.text: This was the first pagoda i've explored in Phu Quoc. Very calm, clean and beautiful place surrounded by mountains and forrest. Should mention the "string of wishes", where you write your prayers.

reviews.author: M K
reviews.text: Really huge and nice temple. There many stairs, but it doesn’t interfere with enjoying the atmosphere

reviews.author: Drew Esa
reviews.text: There is such a thing as a "sacred place" where the atmosphere is filled with good energy, prayers, strength and kindness. Hộ Quốc Temple is just such a place - full, beautiful, kind. I am not a Buddhist, but I visit Buddhist temples with great interest, and it is easy and pleasant for me. It is great that right now Hộ Quốc Temple is being restored and will be able to receive more pilgrims and guests. All the conditions are there - a huge beautiful area, a souvenir store and even a small cafe. Be careful, the temple is open until 18.00 and you must take off your shoes when entering the temples. Everything else you will now see in the photos and video.

reviews.author: Antonios Katsampoxakis
reviews.text: Most beautiful and serene Bhudist temple,I have ever been to!

reviews.author: Joseph Raczynski
reviews.text: Lovely Buddhist temple overlooking the blue green waters. It's super clean and not terribly busy. Worth a visit.

reviews.author: Camille
reviews.text: Beautiful Buddhist temple in Phu Quoc. Definitely a must see if you have the time. Perfect place to relax, recenter, and experience culture. Location is near the ocean, so you have a beautiful wind and ocean smell throughout the temple that adds to its peacefulness. I was able to do some meditation here and the energy is undeniable. For more travel moments, tips, and Asia recommendations, follow my journey on Instagram @soma.salva and TikTok @timetravelfairy

reviews.author: Oleksii “Coyc” Safonov
reviews.text: Very powerful place! Absolutely must to visit if you’re on phuquoc island

reviews.author: Brandy HK
reviews.text: Definitely a rough ride to get to this temple so I advice you to plan accordingly. Luckily I went with a tour guide so driving arrangements were pre-planned. It is a popular attraction site that has a limited parking area which was already full of tour buses when we arrived so parking was kind of difficult to find. We ended up parking on the side road in front of the temple so we could get off and let the bus driver find a spot as we explored. Once you enter the front entrance, you are welcomed by a Buddha statue, planters filled with lotuses, and two sets of stairs that houses a beautiful artwork with gold dragons amongst a light blue background. It is incredibly beautiful in this section, but if you walk up the stairs, you’ll be at the temple and have a better view of Lady Buddha. Even though this temple is located on the mountain by the sea, it is extremely hot. The trees block the wind flow so the humid air is at a standstill which makes it even hotter. I recommend bringing a fan, wearing a hat, and purchasing some ice cream at the store to keep you cool.

reviews.author: Ihar Beliauski
reviews.text: Thiền viện Trúc Lâm Chùa Hộ Quốc is an incredibly beautiful and peaceful place that left me in awe. Nestled on a hilltop, it offers breathtaking views of the sea, making it one of the most serene spots in Phu Quoc. The temple’s architecture is stunning, and the tranquil atmosphere creates a perfect environment for meditation and reflection. The combination of traditional Vietnamese design and the lush surroundings enhances the beauty of the place. Watching the ocean from the temple grounds feels almost magical. It's a must-visit if you're looking to experience peace and natural beauty.

reviews.author: Hồ Lãnh Minh
reviews.text: Amazing temple, arrive early to avoid crowds. Tour buses full of tourist arrive every few minutes, amazing temple but the worst atmosphere I have experienced inside a temple with no locals there…

reviews.author: 王俊翔
reviews.text: Temple with a good spirit and view! Very easy to parking

reviews.author: Thor Gökhan Erdal
reviews.text: Beautiful, calm and zen temple. Worth visit

reviews.author: Florian Remy
reviews.text: A very peaceful pagoda with a beautiful view on the coast and sea. Some tips for future visitors : - Free parking and free entrance, - Located on the East side of Phu Quoc, so if you're lucky with the weather you can enjoy the sunrise here, - Workers start their shift at 7AM, I would recommend coming before if you want to avoid the noise and the crowd, - It's recommended to cover your legs there.

reviews.author: Vaughan Simmons
reviews.text: Beautiful mountain top temple across 3 areas over looking the water... easy moto ride out there and has vegan food next door aswell... definitely worth a visit if your in the area

reviews.author: bodter
reviews.text: On our visit in the ends of June, this temple was an interesting spiritual surprise for us! We enjoyed every minute we visited it. Smart to book the Grab ride both ways with waiting time, it wasn't easy to get a car up on the mountain.

reviews.author: Vichuda Prom
reviews.text: Ho Quoc Pagoda (also known as Truc Lam Ho Quoc Zen Monastery) is a famous pagoda in Phu Quoc, located in the Southeast of the island, about 20km from Duong Dong center and only about 10km from the airport. This is a prominent destination in the South of Phu Quoc Island , famous for its sacred space, impressive sea view and charming scenery. Located in the Phu Quoc tourism journey combining spirituality - relaxation - nature exploration, Ho Quoc Pagoda not only attracts tourists by its majestic architecture but also by the feeling of peace and relaxation between the mountains and the vast ocean. In the temple grounds, the Ancestor House is a solemn place to worship the Three Holy Ancestors. In addition, the temple also has areas to worship Buddha Shakyamuni, Bodhisattva and Dharma Protectors, in accordance with the typical architecture of Truc Lam Zen Monastery. From the pagoda area, you can admire the immense ocean in front of you, behind you are beautiful green mountains and forests. Visitors can stroll through the large temple grounds, admiring the intricately carved Buddha statues. Every step in this temple in Phu Quoc is a journey to purify the mind, immersing in the pure atmosphere amidst majestic nature. In addition, when coming here, worshiping is an experience not to be missed. You can prepare offerings to pray for good things, and at the same time express respect and gratitude to those who have contributed to the country.

reviews.author: Rose Nguyen
reviews.text: It's a really good place to go even tho it's not my religion. People are really nice and sweet, the structure is really clean, you've got an access to toilet that is kinda important to me ! I recommend this place ! Ps: there is a lot of stairs you need a good cardio ;)

reviews.author: Cuynu
reviews.text: biggest temple in phu quoc, just built a few years ago, came on 2024/06/27, im also buddhism and like quiet places just like my mind.

reviews.author: Adilet Saparbekov
reviews.text: truly interesting temple, if i’m not mistaken there is bishamonten

reviews.author: Casey Ka
reviews.text: The most beautiful Pagoda/Temple in phu quoc. Such a beautiful nature viewing of the Temple, Sky, and the Ocean. The breath of natural scenery is so peaceful and relaxing here.

reviews.author: Vivika Ward
reviews.text: Very lovely temple. Worth a stop, if you are travelling to Sao or Khem beach. Easy access from the main road with the motorbike. On the way to the temple there are several beaches where locals go to swim. Unfortunately quite dirty. Temple has plenty of parking places. Very relaxing and calm. U can borrow some robes for free if u are wearing shorts. Entry is also free. We visited in the evening, it was very nice and calm, only few other tourists and monks. Beautiful view overlooking the sea! Even saw a turtle in the pond.

reviews.author: Michel Corrent
reviews.text: Very nice recently pagoda (2012). The area is very quiete and relaxing and also good for who wants to pray. The view is also impressive!

reviews.author: G Whizz
reviews.text: This stunning place called Ho Quoc Pagoda or Ho Quoc Temple (Truc Lam Ho Quoc Monastery) is the largest temple complex on Phu Quoc Island and in the Mekong Delta. It features traditional Ly and Tran dynasty architecture, including a grand triple-arched gate, a large jade Buddha statue, and a dragon staircase. The pagoda is known for its stunning location, with mountains at its back and the ocean in front, offering breathtaking views. It is a significant spiritual and tourist destination, located about 20 kilometers from Duong Dong Town in Suoi Lon Hamlet, Duong To Commune. Well worth the time to see all parts of it.

reviews.author: Nelson Ferreira
reviews.text: Amazing place to visit . Very impressive
